name: Trehalose activated TFEB and improved lysosomal storage-disease model outcomes
summary: Trehalose/TFEB evidence supports pharmacological tractability of lysosomal clearance, but it is disease-model evidence rather than normal aging.
statement: Trehalose activated TFEB through Akt modulation, enhanced lysosomal clearance in disease cells, and in a Batten disease mouse model reduced storage burden, reduced neuropathology, and prolonged survival.
population_or_model: CLN3 Batten disease mouse model and lysosomal-disease patient cells
caveats: Batten disease is a neurodegenerative storage-disease model, not normal aging.
Trehalose is a broad autophagy and metabolic intervention rather than a selective lysosomal restoration therapy.
The finding supports tractability but should not be treated as human aging efficacy.
